name: Brooklyn

ingredients.ingredient: Amer Picon (or sub Amaro Nonino/Ramazzotti + orange bitters)

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice until well-chilled. Strain into chilled coupe.

history: A Manhattan variation published in Jacob Grohusko's 'Jack's Manual' (1908). The Brooklyn is the less famous sibling of the Manhattan — both named for New York boroughs — with dry vermouth instead of sweet and the addition of maraschino and the now-difficult-to-find Amer Picon. Picon approximations are common today. It's a drier, more austere cocktail than the Manhattan.
